segments.text: Thank you.
Thank you.
Tonight's guest is Dave Weigert.
Dave, welcome to the show.
Thank you, Vic.
Thank you for having me on.
Well, thanks so much for coming on.
We appreciate your time.
Dave, please give us a brief bio on yourself.
Yes, my name is Dave Weigert.
And as far as my work background, I worked for Baltimore County Fire and Police.
and I did a lot of private security as well.
Private security?
I can only imagine the stories you have to share.
Yeah, there's a lot.
Oh, I'll bet.
I'm sure there are.
Dave, the first time we spoke,
you told me that dogmen are constantly on your mind.
To what extent?
It's daily.
When I wake up in the morning,
after I get myself started,
it just pops in my head it hasn't gone away since the two encounters so i would say yeah on a daily
basis it's like you know just pops in and out of my mind and i could be sitting there watching tv
and it could just pop up in my mind and i'll sit there and i'll just i'll go through it and
then my mind will drift off to uh thinking about something else
Well, that doesn't make any difference for most eyewitnesses.
I mean, most of them think about their encounters on a daily basis, but that really doesn't
come as a surprise when you have such a profound experience.
That's only natural and it's expected.
In August of 2020, you and your wife moved.
Where'd you go and why'd you move?
Well, we moved from Baltimore, Maryland.
My wife had a massive heart attack.
And after she spent 52 days in the hospital, we went back and stayed at our house for a while.
And then my son said, I think it's time that you move up here where I'm at.
He lived in Dublin, PA.
So he said it would be better for you to move up here.
I can take care of you guys and you can spend more time with your grandchildren.
So we moved up to Dublin, PA with my son.
How's your wife now?
She's doing a lot better.
She still has illnesses such as dementia, and she's dealing with that.
But as far as her heart, that's been doing pretty good.
Well, it goes without saying, I'm so glad to hear her heart's doing better, but I'm so sorry to hear about the dementia.
Yeah, it's really rough.
Oh, I'm sure it is.
Yeah, that can't be easy.
Both of your encounters happened at the cap and you and your wife moved into.
How hospitable would you say the area around it is for dogmen?
Well, there's a lot of farm area around.
There's a lot of woods.
I'm in like a circle.
So I would say there's woods all the way around me,
all the way around.
And there's a lot of wildlife, there's bear, there's deer,
there's a fox, coyotes, all that stuff.
So, you know, from time to time, you might see it,
a skunk a raccoon a fox or something like that on the property So like I said there a lot of wood so there a lot of places for wildlife to live in
Sounds like a great place to live. I don't blame you for moving there.
Both of the dogmen you've encountered could have harmed you, but didn't.
Does that make you think the threat that they pose on average is overrated,
or do you think most of them do pose a real threat?
I know nothing about Dogman
You know, I've read a lot of reports
Of some people that were attacked
And most of them not
So
It's hard to say what Dogman would do
And
I can tell you there's
So many things that went through my mind
After the situation about
What could have possibly happened
But didn't
And I'm so grateful for that
Oh, I'm sure you are. I don't blame you. Your second encounter frightened you so much you froze, and understandably so. Have you had any other experiences that frightened you as much as it did?
No, uh-uh, no. The second encounter, when I experienced that, my legs blocked up first,
I had cramps in my calves, and then the rest of my body just tightened up and that was it.
I couldn't even really talk.
Oh, I'm sure you couldn't. Yeah, that must have been awfully frightening.
Your son was with you when you had the first one, though.
What kind of an effect did it have on him?
Well, to this day, he still gets irritable when I bring it up, when I mention it to him.
He just plain out tells me, I don't want to talk about it.
Forget it. Forget about it.
He's in denial then.
Does he avoid going into the woods now?
I don't think he's gone into the woods since then
He's not a hunter
He doesn't like to walk trails or anything like that
Or go hiking
He really doesn't have a reason to go into the woods
But he's talking about moving now to
A house he wants to buy that has farmland and woods
And I'm going to try to touch bases with him on that
but I don't know how that's going to go.
Hard to say how that's going to pan out.
How about you?
Have your encounters removed any love that you have for being out in the woods
or do you still like spending time out there?
I'm okay with it.
I've always told people I'm a man and I fear no man.
I fear nothing.
I put my pants on the same way as another man.
But when it comes to something like Dogman or something like that, it's hard to say until the time if you encounter something like that, like I did, you can say, oh, I wouldn't be scared of it.
But I was terrified.
I wasn't scared.
I was terrified.
That's a good point.
It's all too easy to have this idea of how you'd handle a Dogman encounter if you had one.
But everyone has a plan until they have a Dogman encounter.
you shared your encounters with one of your neighbors from what i understand how do you
respond i'm gonna say i was laughed at a little bit all right you know because he had mentioned
to me he's like what are you smoking i'm like i'm not smoking anything i don't smoke
he says the only thing i see around here is wildlife that's it and he said that's
Not very often, just from time to time.
It's too bad you responded that way, but as I'm sure you know, most people when you're
approaching with an experience like that, that's what you're going to get.
So it's a shame, but that's how it normally goes.
If you've had a dogman encounter and would like to speak with me about it, whether in
private or on the show, please go to dogmanencounters.com and submit a report.
If you've had a Bigfoot sighting and would like to be a guest on one of my two Bigfoot
shows, please go to MyBigfootSighting.com and let me know.
All right, Dave, please tell us about your encounters now.
Give us every last detail that comes to mind.
In August of 2020, my son and I had a conversation about moving up to the PA area.
So my wife and I, we gathered our stuff and we moved to the PA area and we went to live with our son and his family for about two weeks.
And then we started looking around and I came across the cabin and I went and looked at it and I told my son I said I like it and I think it would be good for us It would work out fine
So we got the cabin, got the keys to it. On August 7th of 2020, my son came to me and said,
look, I'm not ready to kick you out yet. He said, but let's start moving some things into the cabin
so that we can start getting it ready for you.
And then we'll go shopping and we'll get some more stuff
and then we'll take that over to the cabin.
So on August 7th, it was around evening time, almost nighttime.
When we arrived, we pulled into the driveway.
It was dark out.
And I noticed something.
I said to my son, I said, man, it's really dark out here.
I said, the only light we have is coming from up the street,
across the street from a house next to a farm.
and the little bit of moonlight.
He says, yeah.
He says, you might want to get some more lights.
I says, yeah.
So we get out of the vehicle and we go in and we unlock the cabin
and we come back to the SUV and we're at the rear of it
and pulled the dolly out, set it on the ground.
And we both reached back inside to get boxes.
and as we were coming around from out of the back of the SUV,
we sat the boxes down on the ground at the same time
and then our head came up
and what I had saw was a black-looking creature dog
crawling on all fours like the military does
when they're going underneath the wires or whatever they have to crawl under.
And we look at each other and he goes, what?
And we turned back to look at it and it was spinning around as it spun around.
It took off.
And all we saw was it run for a couple of feet and then it just gone into the nighttime because like I said, it was very, very dark out there.
So we got the boxes.
We got him into the cabin.
And he had mentioned before we got there that he wanted to do a total of three loads.
So we got the first load into the cabin.
And as we're driving back to his house, I started talking about what we had saw.
And he was like, do me a favor.
When we get to the house, don't say nothing around the kids and don't say nothing around my girlfriend.
I said, okay.
I said, well, what do you think you saw?
He said, just don't worry about it.
leave it alone. I'm like, okay. So I didn't bother him. So we got back to the house
and he sat down and he just kept sitting there and sitting there and he's watching TV. And I said,
we're going to go back to the cabin and take the other two loads. He says, no, we'll do it tomorrow.
I said, okay. So I went up and got ready for bed and I went to bed that night and
And got up the next day and I tried to talk to him about it some more.
And he got upset and just told me, drop it, leave it alone.
And I'm like, okay.
So me and my wife finally moved into the cabin and we're now settled.
and seven months later on March 1st of 2021,
I'm sitting in the house and I could hear the wind kicking up.
So I turned the news on and I'm watching the weather
and they're calling for 40 to 50 mile an hour winds.
You could hear chimes moving around.
You can hear people's belongings sliding,
such as boxes and whatever people had under their carport and outdoor.
So I told my wife, I said, well, you know, I said, tomorrow's trash.
I got to put recycling.
I got to put the trash out.
And she said, well, why don't you wait until morning?
I said, no, I'm going to do it now and get it over with.
So I took the two trash cans down to the end of the driveway.
I sat them there.
And I come back in and we watched some TV and it started getting late
So around 11 o'clock, she says, I'm going to go to bed. I'm tired. I said, okay
So I stayed up and I watched some more TV
And then I said, I'm going to get on the computer
So I got on the computer and I'm listening to music from YouTube and looking at YouTube videos
and you know just browsing and looking at different things So I took my headphones off went to the bathroom I come back I got me a drink
I sat back down, I put the headphones on for a little bit more,
listening to some more music.
And I looked at the clock and I'm like, oh, my Lord, it's three o'clock in the morning.
I better get ready for bed.
So I took the headphones off.
Just as I took the headphones off, I heard a loud bang, boom.
I'm like, oh, Lord, please tell me it wasn't the trash can.
Because I was worried about the lid, you know, if it blew over, the lid flipping up and the stuff coming out and being on the street.
So I got my headlamp that I had bought for when I go outside at night.
I put that on, I put my jacket on and I got my slippers and I went down to the end of the driveway.
And I'm like, yeah, one of them blew over.
So I get up to the trash cans and I'm leaning over and I'm grabbing a hold of the trash can.
When I went to pull it up, I started hearing a car horn on and off.
like somebody was just like laying on a horn, letting it go,
laying on the horn, letting it go.
And then all of a sudden I hear the car's brakes locked up.
When I looked up to the top of the street,
because I was expecting a car accident.
When I looked up to the top of my street, it's in the shape of a T.
You can go left, you can go right, and you can come into my street.
And if you look straight ahead,
you're looking at a house that sits next to a farm and it's a good distance across that farm
before you get into the woods. So there's a little light on the side of the house
and it pretty much lights up part of the street. So at first I thought I was seeing
a person just standing there. And then the car finally came to a stop and it's still blowing
its horn and i'm like that's not a person it dropped to all fours and started running down
the street towards me down my street and as soon as it got to about 12 to 15 feet for me it stood
up on its hind legs its front paws started boxing like like a horse would if it got on its hind legs
Its two legs would look like it's kind of boxing.
So I grabbed a hold of the handle with both hands.
It was a trash can.
This thing had yellow eyes.
Its teeth were out of this world.
It had claws, long claws, big, thick claws.
It had black hair.
It was spiky looking.
Its sides were moving in and out
It was growling a little bit
And foaming from the bottom of its mouth
And I just like totally locked up
The calves in my legs locked up
And then my body followed right behind it and just stiffened up
I couldn't move
Suddenly it dropped to all fours
Ran in the same direction as the first encounter and gone in a matter of seconds.
Now, going down my street at the very end, it turns into like a circle.
It dead ends.
There's a house that sits at the bottom.
There's houses on the left and there's houses on the right.
They're all single homes with woods behind them.
so when you get to the very bottom that house has woods behind it so i'm assuming
that's where both of them had ran to at that point the guy peels wheels
he turns he comes down his window comes down and he says what was that
and I tried to talk. I couldn't do anything but mumble. My legs were still locked. My body was
still stiff. So the window goes back up and he takes off and goes down in the direction where
it had ran to. By then, I'm trying to get myself together so I could get in the cabin.
and I managed to start backing up and as I'm backing up
I walked
into the tree that's in the front of my cabin in the grass area.
My back touched it and I jumped.
When I jumped, it kind of like helped me a little bit to be able to start moving a little quicker.
So I'm still walking backwards, but not taking my eyes off the road.
So I managed to get to the cabin.
I opened the screen door.
I went inside.
I pulled the screen door shut, I locked it, I reached
my file cabinet and I got out my gun. I stood there
and I said to myself, I wonder if this guy's going to come up
and want to talk to me. He comes flying
back up the road, he stops, his window comes
down, he goes and tries to ask me a question, his
phone rings. So he gets on his phone and he's explaining to someone
on what had just happened.
And I think they were making fun of him on the phone because he started
yelling at him and telling him things like, no, I'm serious.
It did happen.
And then he just drove off and made a left and I never seen him again.
So, you know, I shut the door and I sat down at my computer.
I'm scratching my head.
I'm like, what the heck just happened to me?
and I didn't want to go wake my wife up, you know, because it was way after 3 a.m.
So I get on a computer, and I started researching.
I started looking.
I'm looking up black dogs.
So I started coming across stuff like dog man, black creature, dog-looking creatures.
And so I couldn't sleep the rest of the night.
I didn't get to sleep until daybreak.
I was up when the trash man came.
I didn't even go out and get the cans.
I just went to bed right after that.
And that was it.
And then, you know, it's been on my mind ever since.
And I don't plan on going anywhere.
So I'm just praying that I don't have a third encounter.
And within two blocks from where I live, there's been Bigfoot sightings.
Recently, there's been a whole mess of UFO sightings in Bucks County.
Every time you turn around, you're reading in the town paper that there's been a new UFO sighting.
And I still go online and look to see if there's been any recent dogman sightings.
And I can't get it out of my mind.
I mean, I'm not losing sleep, but I get up and it's in my mind on a daily basis.
Before August 7th of 2020, what were your thoughts on the existence of cryptids like Dogman and Sasquatch, Dave?
Well, here's something interesting.
In 2003, I created a paranormal group.
And I was a paranormal investigator.
We just strictly did paranormal sightings, ghost related.
And I had that team until 2016.
And then I started having some health issues.
So I decided to close the group down and slow down and do something else with my life.
But never have I ever experienced anything like I did here.
Oh, I'm sure, yeah, it'd be hard to duplicate.
How far would you say that first dogman was from you when you first saw it army crawling
the way you did?
Well, let's see.
The SUV comes into the driveway, so that would leave about, I guess, I'm going to say 15,
20 feet, because I do have a long, pretty long driveway.
driveway goes up underneath the carport, but we weren't pulled completely up to the carport
because we wanted to allow us some room in case we wanted to leave some boxes in the
carport until we got enough room in there and got situated after putting boxes away.
But yeah, I would say no more than 15, 20 feet.
Ooh, that's close.
Yeah, way too close for comfort.
Was it behind you or in front of you?
It was behind us.
Our backs were to it.

And as we reached into the SUV to grab boxes we swung around and then we were looking forward as we were setting the boxes down And like I said when we noticed it both of us at the same time our head came up
And then we stood in an upright position and was like, what the heck was that?
Well, I'm sure you said something worse than that, but.
Yeah, I'll bet.
Was it crawling perpendicular?
To you too, or somewhat towards you?
I don't think it was crawling towards us because it was still over.
It was over the line.
And when I say the line, that's where the driveway divides driveway into the street.
So it was still in the street.
It was over the driveway line.
And it didn't look like it was turning left, which it would have had to if it was coming into the driveway.
It just looked like it was trying to get by unnoticed.

And we startled it.
And it just, you know, like I said, it spun around, gone.

You know, no eye color or anything.
I mean, we noticed it was dog-like looking, but very strange.
You didn't give too many details about how it looked.

The first one, no.
But the second one, I didn't take my eyes off of it.
The first one, it just, it happened even quicker and it was gone faster than the second one.
So there was no room to be able to get a description the first time because it happened so fast.
Well, that's not your fault.
You're just doing the best you could to process that traumatic experience.
so no one could fault you for that.
I'm a little cloudy on this, though.
Where it was, pretty close to where your driveway touched the street,
was it fairly dark right there,
or was there enough light for you to be able to tell if it was ever looking at you,
or do you know?

If it's a full moon out there, you can see a lot better.
If it's cloudy out, you're not going to see that well unless you have a flashlight.
And like I said, the house at the top where the T is, the shape of the street, has a light on the side of the house that only illuminates the street up there.

But from the moonlight, you can see on my street at times depending on the clouds.
Sounds like it was pretty dark over there then. Could you make out enough detail to get a feel for how big it was?
Um, I would say at least four feet long.
Uh, the size of maybe a, gosh, I'm not good with dog names, bigger than a lab, a full grown lab.

It was a lot smaller.

At first, when I saw it, well, we both saw it at the same time,

It wasn't a, you know, a pet dog.
Sure would have been nice if it was just a coyote.

On March 1st of 2021, when you had that second encounter, you said that the loud bang you heard was why you went outside.

I still couldn't be able to tell because from the front door, let's see, that's about, I would say, 30 feet to the trash can.

I'll bet it was just the wind blowing that can over and that's all there was to it.

As I was walking across the grass, I hadn't yet heard the car, the car horn.
From my side vision, I didn't see anything up at the top of the street where that little bit of light lights up the street.

That's what made me look up to the top of the street.
When I looked up to the top of the street, it was standing on two legs, but I wasn't sure.
I wasn't clear what it was.
I mean, at first, you know, my first instinct was, is that a person just standing in the street?

The car horn and then the brakes locking up and the car coming to a stop.
And the thing did not move until the car came to a complete stop.
And I would say the car, the front of the car was maybe six feet from it.
And then it just dropped.

i was concerned about him too because he didn't look like an everyday

that second one did it.
